title: Add verify CLI command to cli.py
description: Add the verify Click command to pypptx/cli.py. The command accepts a .pptx file path argument, calls verify_pptx() from pypptx.ops.verify, and formats output via the existing output_result() helper. JSON output (default) includes errors, warnings, passed (bool: errors is empty), slide_count, error_count, and warning_count. --plain output writes one line per issue prefixed with FAIL for errors and WARN for warnings, with no output when the file is clean. Exit code 0 when errors list is empty; exit code 1 when errors are present (use sys.exit or click's standalone_mode).
acceptance_criteria: pypptx verify presentation.pptx command is registered under the CLI group and callable
JSON output includes errors, warnings, passed, slide_count, error_count, warning_count
--plain output writes FAIL-prefixed lines for errors and WARN-prefixed lines for warnings
--plain output produces no output when there are no issues
Exit code is 0 when errors list is empty (warnings do not affect exit code)
Exit code is 1 when any errors are present
Error messages from file-not-found or pptx parse failures go to stderr

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented `verify_cmd` function registered as `@cli.command("verify")` at .apm/skills/pypptx/pypptx/cli.py:173-215. Command follows all existing CLI patterns (output_result-style logic, --plain flag, error handling to stderr, sys.exit on failure).
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The verify ops module (verify_pptx) was already complete from prior beads (B-966cf34d and dependencies).
handoff_summary.next_action: Pass to tester agent to validate all acceptance criteria against a real .pptx file.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only cli.py was modified; no sibling beads claim this file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Did not use output_result() helper for the verify command because the exit-code logic (sys.exit(1) when errors exist) needed to fire after output was written, and the plain output must produce no output at all when clean (not an empty string followed by newline). Inlined the JSON/plain branching directly in verify_cmd to maintain correct exit-code sequencing.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should cover: clean file (exit 0, JSON passed=true), file with errors (exit 1, JSON passed=false), --plain with errors+warnings (FAIL/WARN prefixes), --plain with no issues (no output), missing file (stderr error message).

conflict_risks: None — only cli.py was modified; no sibling beads claim this file.

execution_history.summary: Added the `verify` CLI command to `pypptx/cli.py`. The command accepts a .pptx file path, calls `verify_pptx()` from `pypptx.ops.verify`, and formats output via JSON (default) or --plain mode. JSON includes all required fields (errors, warnings, passed, slide_count, error_count, warning_count). --plain writes FAIL/WARN-prefixed lines with no output when clean. Exit code 0 when errors list is empty; exit code 1 when errors are present. File exceptions go to stderr. Syntax check passed.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
